title: analytics — event category taxonomy
description: Closed set of AnalyticsEvent.category values. Each category has a description, a display label, an icon, retention behavior, and default consent requirements.

categories.label: Feature use
categories.description: Feature surfaces viewed / used / dismissed. The largest bucket by volume; captures product engagement patterns.

categories.label: Funnel step
categories.description: Steps in a defined funnel (signup, onboarding, checkout, feature-adoption). Powers the funnel report.
categories.retention_note: Longer retention by default — funnel windows > 1yr are useful for enterprise cohort analysis.

categories.label: Call to action
categories.description: Explicit user actions on interactive surfaces (button clicks, form submits, link follows).

categories.label: Error
categories.description: Errors the user encountered (validation failures, rate limits, application errors). Useful for UX + reliability triage.
categories.retention_note: Shorter retention — error volume is high, decays in usefulness after a quarter.
categories.consent_note: Errors are treated as `functional` consent (they help the platform operate correctly) rather than `analytics`.

categories.label: Performance
categories.description: Timing signals (screen load, API call duration). Similar to monitoring metrics but subject-scoped so we can attribute perf issues to specific users/regions.

categories.label: Experiment
categories.description: A/B experiment variant assignments + exposures. Powers ExperimentAssignmentReportBuilder.
categories.retention_note: Long retention — experiment analysis spans months.

categories.label: Lifecycle
categories.description: User + subscription lifecycle milestones (registered, activated, upgraded, downgraded, churned).
categories.consent_note: Lifecycle events use `essential` consent — they're operationally required (business audit trail).

categories.label: Session
categories.retention_note: Short retention — session-level granularity is aggregated into feature_use + funnel over time.
